justification.personality: The film captures his enthusiastic, slightly overwhelming nature, but the book allows for a broader range of interactions (e.g., with Ginny, the general atmosphere of the school).
justification.narrative_role: His primary role in the film is reduced to a single, dramatic incident (the attack), whereas in the books, he is a more recurring, background fixture of the school life.
justification.motivations: His core motivation—a deep, almost obsessive desire to document and prove he was there—is present, but the book gives him more agency in his interactions.
justification.character_arc: The book shows him interacting with different characters and events over time, suggesting a more natural, if minor, growth. The film focuses almost entirely on the inciting incident of the attack, limiting any visible arc.

comparative.key_observations: The film significantly streamlines his presence, focusing almost entirely on the single, dramatic event of his attack, losing the sense of his persistent, background presence seen in the books.
